issues.body: The added HEAVY_MODULE_SUFFIXES entry passes only if the real `next build` emits a react-loadable-manifest.json key ending in `-> @/components/repl-graph-view`. Unlike the 8 gallery demos and hero-runner (which use next/dynamic) this module is loaded via `React.lazy(() => import(...))` in hero-runner.tsx:60. Existing precedent strongly suggests this is fine — @aztec/bb.js and fzstd are plain dynamic import() split points already in the list and passing — so plain import() entries do get recorded. This is not a blocker; it is only a note that CI (`npm run check:bundle` / postbuild) is the true confirmation, and if the manifest key format for a `@/`-aliased local behind React.lazy differs, the guard would false-fail with a PRESENCE error rather than silently pass.
issues.fix_hint: No change required if the postbuild `check:bundle` run in CI passes for this branch. If it false-fails, confirm the actual react-loadable-manifest.json key for the repl-graph-view import and adjust the suffix to match the emitted specifier.
issues.title: PRESENCE guard for repl-graph-view relies on the React.lazy plain import() appearing in react-loadable-manifest.json

verdict.summary: Registers @/components/repl-graph-view as a heavy module in the site bundle guard (check-bundle.mjs) and adds a PRESENCE unit test locking in that the hero's Graph view stays a lazy split point (follow-up to #3606). Verified: (1) the component exists at site/src/components/repl-graph-view.tsx and is loaded via React.lazy(() => import("@/components/repl-graph-view")) in home/hero-runner.tsx:60 — a genuine dynamic-import split point, matching the comment's claim that its SVG renderer + deriveGraph load only on Graph-view invocation; (2) the new test is non-vacuous — it asserts membership in HEAVY_MODULE_SUFFIXES, deletes the loadable entry, and requires exactly one PRESENCE error naming the module, mirroring the existing bb.js/fzstd/zk-car-hire PRESENCE tests; (3) healthyInputs() iterates HEAVY_MODULE_SUFFIXES, so adding the module to both list and fixture keeps the healthy-build and heavyReport.length assertions consistent with no drift. Existing plain-import() entries (@aztec/bb.js, fzstd) are already in the list and pass, so the manifest-entry precedent covers this new plain import() too. No security/soundness/injection concerns. Correct and complete.
